mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) across 100+ easing observations, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained unemployment rate above 8.5 percent signals severe labor market deterioration that crushes consumer spending expectations and forces equity repricing downward within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 8.5%, or the signal loses predictive power when inflation exceeds 5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand recovery, driving corporate earnings revisions and generating positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day returns in >60% of cases, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in OLS regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production contraction during late-cycle expansions indicates capacity constraints and supply chain bottlenecks that trigger short-term profit margin compression and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive or flat when INDPRO declines during late-cycle phases, or the mechanism fails to separate from general market beta in multivariate tests.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rates exceeding 4.0 percent impose severe financing constraints on highly leveraged sectors, triggering risk-off flows and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 20-day returns in >50% of observations when FEDFUNDS surpasses 4.0%, or the threshold effect vanishes when GDP growth exceeds 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total employment combined with falling unemployment rates confirms robust labor market tightening that supports wage growth and consumer confidence, lifting equity valuations over 20 days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 20-day returns in >65% of cases, or the interaction effect is fully subsumed by single-series predictors in stepwise regression.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly translates to expanded corporate revenue bases and improved earnings forecasts, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show zero or negative correlation with GDPC1 revisions over rolling 5-year windows, or Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production rebounds above 90 index points during post-shock recovery phases signal rapid capacity utilization restoration, triggering short-term momentum buying and positive 5-day equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day returns are negative in >55% of instances when INDPRO crosses above 90 during recovery regimes, or the signal loses significance when volatility indices exceed 25.
